results.editorial:
Persatuan Kebajikan Rumah Jagaan VCFU is a registered welfare-society nursing home in Klang, operating from No. 65, Lorong Mohd Tahir 6B, Taman Saujana. It is registered with Jabatan Kebajikan Masyarakat under licence B/PJB WT 015/2017 and runs as a non-profit welfare association rather than a private commercial chain. The home is reachable on 03-3372 5888 and 010-213 0256, and on email at vc4uhomeklang@gmail.com. Note that the website occasionally cited for this facility (srieden.org) belongs to an unrelated children's special-needs organisation and should not be used as the source of truth for VCFU.

As a welfare-society home in suburban Klang, VCFU is set up for general residential elder care — long-term stay for older adults whose families cannot provide round-the-clock supervision at home. Detailed clinical capabilities, staffing ratios, and the precise mix of mobile, semi-dependent, and bedridden residents are not published on any verified channel, so families should confirm directly whether the home accepts cases requiring tube feeding, oxygen, wound dressing, or stroke rehabilitation. Pricing is not publicly listed and is set on assessment.

For families considering VCFU, the practical steps are to phone ahead, request the JKM operating licence and a written fee breakdown, and visit in person before committing. Ask specifically about nurse-to-resident ratio overnight, how medication rounds are documented, and what happens when a resident becomes acutely unwell — which hospital they are sent to, who escorts, and whether the family is contacted before transfer. A welfare-society structure can mean lower fees than commercial homes, but it also means scrutinising staffing and medical backup is more important, not less.

facts.red_flags: Listed website srieden.org belongs to an unrelated children's special-needs charity, not this nursing home. No verified clinical capability or staffing detail published.

results.editorial:
Ummi Naura Care Centre (UNCC), also styled Pusat Jagaan dan Pendidikan Warga Emas, is a Muslim-oriented residential care centre in Muar, Johor. It operates from No. 19, Jalan Murni 15, Taman Putera Murni, off Jalan Salleh, 84000 Muar, and is contactable on 013-979 6861 or umminauracarecentre@gmail.com. The centre began operations in 2019 and is registered with Jabatan Kebajikan Masyarakat. Its stated scope is 24-hour residential care for older adults including stroke survivors, bedridden and paralysed residents, abandoned elderly, cancer patients, persons with disabilities (OKU), and mental-health patients whose condition is controlled.

Day-to-day care is delivered by nursing staff described by the centre as experienced in handling dependent residents. The current admission policy as published by the home is male residents only, on either permanent or short-term placement. The centre sits in the Muslim social-care tradition — the name Ummi Naura and its activity calendar (e.g. Ramadhan infaq drives, donation campaigns through ToyyibPay and Sumbangan.com) signal a Muslim, Malay-medium environment, though formal halal certification status is not separately published.

Families considering UNCC should confirm bed availability for the specific dependency level needed (ambulant vs bedridden vs PEG-fed), ask for the JKM licence number in writing, and clarify what is included in the monthly fee versus charged extra (diapers, milk feeds, dressings, doctor visits, hospital escort). Because the centre takes mental-health and OKU cases alongside frail elderly, it is reasonable to ask how residents are grouped by ward and how behaviourally complex residents are managed. Visit before signing.

facts.red_flags: Currently admits male residents only. Mixed acuity including controlled mental-health cases — confirm grouping arrangements.

results.editorial:
EHA Lakeview Eldercare Mansion is the Permas Jaya branch of EHA Eldercare Group, a Malaysian eldercare operator (registration 1478860-U) that grew out of the former Justlife ElderCare Home brand established in 2009. The Lakeview branch is at 25, Jalan Bayu Puteri 3/1, Taman Bayu Puteri, 81750 Johor Bahru, on a lakeside plot in Permas Jaya. Enquiries go through 019-728 1686 locally or the group careline 019-728 3697, and email ask.ehagroup@gmail.com. The group operates eight residential locations across Johor and the Klang Valley and reports more than 100 caregivers serving 500-plus residents across the network.

At a group level, EHA's published service mix is long-term assisted senior living, respite stays of one to six months, and a five-day day-care programme running roughly 11 hours daily. Standard scope across branches includes skilled nursing procedures, physiotherapy, dementia care, and meal planning by on-site cooks. Branch-specific bed counts, exact nurse-to-resident ratios, and pricing are not published on the EHA website and need to be requested directly. The Lakeview setting itself — a quiet residential pocket in Permas Jaya with a lake frontage — is the differentiator most cited by visitors.

For families shortlisting Lakeview, the useful questions are concrete: how many beds in single, twin and shared rooms; whether the branch handles PEG feeding, suction, oxygen, and post-stroke rehab on site or refers out; whether a registered nurse is rostered overnight (versus on-call); the closest hospital used for emergencies (Hospital Sultan Ismail and KPJ Puteri are both within reach); and what the published fee covers versus billed extras such as diapers, milk feeds, transport, and physiotherapy sessions. Ask to see the JKM operating licence on visit.

facts.red_flags: Branch-specific bed count, staffing ratio and pricing not published — must be confirmed directly.

results.editorial:
EHA Golfview Eldercare Mansion is the Johor Jaya branch of EHA Eldercare Group, sited unusually within the grounds of Daiman 18 Golf Club at 85, Jalan Pesona, Taman Johor Jaya, 81100 Johor Bahru. Phone lines are 019-728 3697 and 019-772 3697, with email at ask.ehagroup@gmail.com and visiting hours 9am-6pm daily. The parent group, EHA Eldercare (1478860-U), evolved from the Justlife ElderCare Home brand founded in 2009 and now runs eight residential locations across Malaysia with a combined 100-plus caregivers and 500-plus residents.

Golfview's published profile emphasises landscaped grounds and lounge space inside the golf-club premises, on-site meal preparation tailored to individual dietary needs, and a 24/7 caregiving roster. Group-level services that apply across branches include long-term assisted living, respite stays of one to six months, day care, physiotherapy, dementia care, and skilled nursing procedures. The branch is associated with post-stroke rehabilitation in third-party listings, but the EHA website itself does not publish stroke-specific protocols, therapist staffing, or rehab equipment lists, so this should be verified on visit. Bed count and pricing are not posted publicly.

For families looking at Golfview, the practical checklist is the same regardless of the marketing: ask for the JKM licence on viewing, request a written fee schedule with all add-ons spelled out, and clarify medical capability item by item — PEG feeds, suction, oxygen concentrator, wound dressings, urinary catheter changes, and what is referred out. If post-stroke rehab is the reason for choosing this branch, ask how often a physiotherapist attends in person, who supervises home-programme exercises on non-therapist days, and what outcome measures (transfers, gait, ADL) the home tracks. Hospital Sultan Ismail is the nearest tertiary centre.

facts.red_flags: Post-stroke specialisation appears in listings but is not detailed on the EHA website. Bed count and pricing not published.
